changes_made: Changed k8s_audit_logging_enabled to k8s_apiserver_audit_logging_enabled for specificity and consistency
review_notes: The functions generally map well to Kubernetes security controls relevant to ISO27001 A.8.9 Configuration Management. However, the audit logging function needed refinement to specify it pertains to the API server, aligning with Kubernetes terminology and ensuring clarity.
naming_improvements: Ensure all functions specify the relevant component, such as 'apiserver' for audit logging, to avoid ambiguity.
title: Configuration Management
step1_reasoning: ISO27001 A.8.9 Configuration Management requires ensuring secure configurations across systems. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to several security domains: RBAC for access control to ensure least privilege, NetworkPolicies to restrict ingress/egress traffic, Secrets management for encryption at rest, and Audit logging for tracking configuration changes and access.
